stimulus: Researchers found that providing rankings biased participants’ attention toward top-ranked sites.
stem: Which choice best describes data in the table that support the researchers’ conclusion?

answerOptions.content: Participants who were not provided with a ranking spent the most time reading about the Roman Forum.
Participants who were provided with a ranking spent the least amount of time reading about the Pantheon.
Participants who were provided with a ranking spent disproportionately more time reading about the Colosseum.
Participants who were not provided with a ranking spent roughly equal amounts of time reading about each site.

rationale:
Choice C is correct because the ranking group spent 9.2 minutes on the top-ranked Colosseum, far more than on the others (2.1, 1.6, 1.2, 1.0), directly supporting the claim that rankings biased attention toward the top-ranked site.

Choice A is incorrect: the no-ranking group spent roughly equal time across all sites, not the most on the Roman Forum. Choice B is incorrect: the ranking group spent the least time on the Spanish Steps (1.0), not the Pantheon. Choice D is incorrect: even though the no-ranking group did spend roughly equal time across sites, this fact by itself doesn’t show that rankings biased the other group.
